title: Align .rules and zed-agentops-init.sh startup contract with workspace initialization
summary: Define the required changes to the repository .rules file and the scaffolded .rules heredoc in zed-agentops-init.sh so startup mandates a one-time workspace_initialize(cwd) call before any canonical state restore or other root-dependent work begins.
acceptance_criteria: Specifies that `workspace_initialize(cwd)` must be the first mandatory startup step before reading `tx_state`, `tx_event_log`, or `handoff`.
Defines consistent wording updates for both the repository `.rules` file and the scaffolded `.rules` heredoc in `zed-agentops-init.sh`.
Clarifies that root-dependent tools must not run before workspace initialization completes successfully.
Keeps the startup contract aligned with the one-project-per-server invariant and the safety rule that `/.agent/` must never be used as the artifact root.

steps.description: Review the current Start (mandatory) wording in the repository .rules file and in the zed-agentops-init.sh heredoc.
Define the new startup order so workspace_initialize(cwd) runs exactly once before canonical state restore.
Document required wording for initialization, no-op same-root reinitialization, and rejection of root-dependent work before initialization.
Record the alignment requirements so the repository .rules file and scaffold output remain synchronized.

notes: This ticket is contract/spec alignment only; it does not implement runtime changes by itself.
The startup wording should support AI-agent compliance, but runtime enforcement remains necessary for safety.
The scaffolded heredoc and repository .rules file must be updated together to avoid drift.
